item.action: Aggregate privacy-safe OIDC temporal observations into a minimum-cohort calibration summary without retaining identity-bearing claims or automatically changing verifier policy.

progress.finding: Privacy-safe per-run observations still require a cohort boundary and an explicit no-auto-policy-change rule. Aggregate temporal statistics are useful for review, while raw tokens and identity-bearing claims remain prohibited.
progress.verification: 5 deterministic Node tests passed before commit from byte-identical files.
progress.uncertainty: No live GitHub OIDC observations were sampled, and a five-observation threshold is an engineering safeguard rather than a universal statistical guarantee.

item.handoff: Cloudflare Research should aggregate only independently verified R-008 observations, retain the R-009 summary as evidence for human review, and keep verifier policy changes as a separate explicit decision with rollback and fresh validation.

reason: Shared queue state is maintained through additive projections to avoid overwriting concurrent team work.
